Name: USkeletalMeshComponent
Description:
SkeletalMeshComponent is used to create an instance of an animated SkeletalMesh asset.
 
  @see USkeletalMesh

Variables.Name: AnimationMode
AnimClass
Variables.Description: The AnimBlueprint class to use. Use 'SetAnimInstanceClass' to change at runtime.

Variables.Name: bAutoInitAnimInstance
Variables.Description: The AnimBlueprint class to use. Use 'SetAnimInstanceClass' to change at runtime.

Variables.Name: AnimScriptInstance
Variables.Description: The active animation graph program instance.

Variables.Name: SubInstances
Variables.Description: Any running sub anim instances that need to be updates on the game thread

Variables.Name: PostProcessAnimInstance
Variables.Description:
An instance created from the PostPhysicsBlueprint property of the skeletal mesh we're using,
	   Runs after physics has been blended

Variables.Name: CachedBoneSpaceTransforms
Variables.Description: Cached BoneSpaceTransforms for Update Rate optimization.

Variables.Name: CachedComponentSpaceTransforms
Variables.Description: Cached SpaceBases for Update Rate optimization.

Variables.Name: GlobalAnimRateScale
Variables.Description: Used to scale speed of all animations on this skeletal mesh.

Variables.Name: UseAsyncScene
Variables.Description: The simulation scene to use for this instance. By default we use what's in the physics asset (which defaults to the sync scene)

Variables.Name: bHasValidBodies
Variables.Description: If true, there is at least one body in the current PhysicsAsset with a valid bone in the current SkeletalMesh

Variables.Name: KinematicBonesUpdateType
Variables.Description: If we are running physics, should we update non-simulated bones based on the animation bone positions.

Variables.Name: PhysicsTransformUpdateMode
Variables.Description: Whether physics simulation updates component transform.

Variables.Name: bBlendPhysics
Variables.Description: Enables blending in of physics bodies whether Simulate or not

Variables.Name: bEnablePhysicsOnDedicatedServer
Variables.Description:
If true, simulate physics for this component on a dedicated server.
	   This should be set if simulating physics and replicating with a dedicated server.
	 	Note: This property cannot be changed at runtime.

Variables.Name: bUpdateJointsFromAnimation
Variables.Description:
If we should pass joint position to joints each frame, so that they can be used by motorized joints to drive the
	 	ragdoll based on the animation.

Variables.Name: bDisableClothSimulation
Variables.Description: Disable cloth simulation and play original animation without simulation

Variables.Name: bAllowAnimCurveEvaluation
Variables.Description: Disable animation curves for this component. If this is set true, no curves will be processed

Variables.Name: bDisableAnimCurves_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: DEPRECATED. Use bAllowAnimCurveEvaluation instead

Variables.Name: DisallowedAnimCurves
Variables.Description:
You can choose to disable certain curves if you prefer.
	  This is transient curves that will be ignored by animation system if you choose this

Variables.Name: bCollideWithEnvironment
Variables.Description: can't collide with part of environment if total collision volumes exceed 16 capsules or 32 planes per convex

Variables.Name: bCollideWithAttachedChildren
Variables.Description: can't collide with part of attached children if total collision volumes exceed 16 capsules or 32 planes per convex

Variables.Name: bLocalSpaceSimulation
Variables.Description:
It's worth trying this option when you feel that the current cloth simulation is unstable.
	  The scale of the actor is maintained during the simulation.
	  It is possible to add the inertia effects to the simulation, through the inertiaScale parameter of the clothing material.
	  So with an inertiaScale of 1.0 there should be no visible difference between local space and global space simulation.
	  Known issues: - Currently there's simulation issues when this feature is used in 3.x (DE4076) So if localSpaceSim is enabled there's no inertia effect when the global pose of the clothing actor changes.

Variables.Name: bClothMorphTarget
Variables.Description:
cloth morph target option
	  This option will be applied only before playing because should do pre-calculation to reduce computation time for run-time play
	  so it's impossible to change this option in run-time

Variables.Name: bResetAfterTeleport
Variables.Description: reset the clothing after moving the clothing position (called teleport)

Variables.Name: ClothBlendWeight
Variables.Description:
weight to blend between simulated results and key-framed positions
	  if weight is 1.0, shows only cloth simulation results and 0.0 will show only skinned results

Variables.Name: RootBoneTranslation
Variables.Description: Offset of the root bone from the reference pose. Used to offset bounding box.

Variables.Name: bDeferMovementFromSceneQueries
Variables.Description:
Optimization
	 
	  Whether animation and world transform updates are deferred. If this is on, the kinematic bodies (scene query data) will not update until the next time the physics simulation is run

Variables.Name: bNoSkeletonUpdate
Variables.Description: Skips Ticking and Bone Refresh.

Variables.Name: bPauseAnims
Variables.Description: pauses this component's animations (doesn't tick them, but still refreshes bones)

Variables.Name: bUseRefPoseOnInitAnim
Variables.Description: On InitAnim should we set to ref pose (if false use first tick of animation data)

Variables.Name: bEnablePerPolyCollision
Variables.Description: Uses skinned data for collision data.

Variables.Name: BodySetup
Variables.Description:
Used for per poly collision. In 99% of cases you will be better off using a Physics Asset.
	 This BodySetup is per instance because all modification of vertices is done in place

Variables.Name: bForceRefpose
Variables.Description:
Misc
	 
	 If true, force the mesh into the reference pose - is an optimization.

Variables.Name: bOnlyAllowAutonomousTickPose
Variables.Description:
If true TickPose() will not be called from the Component's TickComponent function.
	 It will instead be called from Autonomous networking updates. See ACharacter.

Variables.Name: bIsAutonomousTickPose
Variables.Description: True if calling TickPose() from Autonomous networking updates. See ACharacter.

Variables.Name: bOldForceRefPose
Variables.Description: If bForceRefPose was set last tick.

Variables.Name: bShowPrePhysBones
Variables.Description: Bool that enables debug drawing of the skeleton before it is passed to the physics. Useful for debugging animation-driven physics.

Variables.Name: bRequiredBonesUpToDate
Variables.Description: If false, indicates that on the next call to UpdateSkelPose the RequiredBones array should be recalculated.

Variables.Name: bAnimTreeInitialised
Variables.Description: If true, AnimTree has been initialised.

Variables.Name: bIncludeComponentLocationIntoBounds
Variables.Description:
If true, the Location of this Component will be included into its bounds calculation
	 (this can be useful when using SMU_OnlyTickPoseWhenRendered on a character that moves away from the root and no bones are left near the origin of the component)

Variables.Name: bEnableLineCheckWithBounds
Variables.Description: If true, line checks will test against the bounding box of this skeletal mesh component and return a hit if there is a collision.

Variables.Name: CachedAnimCurveUidVersion
Variables.Description: Cache AnimCurveUidVersion from Skeleton and this will be used to identify if it needs to be updated

Variables.Name: LineCheckBoundsScale
Variables.Description: If bEnableLineCheckWithBounds is true, scale the bounds by this value before doing line check.

Variables.Name: OnConstraintBroken
Variables.Description: Notification when constraint is broken.

Variables.Name: ClothingSimulationFactory
Variables.Description: Class of the object responsible for

Variables.Name: TeleportDistanceThreshold
Variables.Description:
Conduct teleportation if the character's movement is greater than this threshold in 1 frame.
	 Zero or negative values will skip the check.
	 You can also do force teleport manually using ForceNextUpdateTeleport()  ForceNextUpdateTeleportAndReset().

Variables.Name: TeleportRotationThreshold
Variables.Description:
Rotation threshold in degrees, ranging from 0 to 180.
	 Conduct teleportation if the character's rotation is greater than this threshold in 1 frame.
	 Zero or negative values will skip the check.

Variables.Name: LastPoseTickTime
Variables.Description: Keep track of when animation has been ticked to ensure it is ticked only once per frame.

Variables.Name: bIsEnableBatchSection
Variables.Description: For Dynamic Bone Scale Feature End

Variables.Name: AnimationBlueprint_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: The blueprint for creating an AnimationScript.

Variables.Name: bUpdateAnimationInEditor
Variables.Description: If true, this will Tick until disabled

Variables.Name: BoneRetargetBaseRefMesh
Variables.Description: For Bone Retarget Feature Start

Functions.Name: GetAnimInstance
Functions.Description:
Returns the animation instance that is driving the class (if available). This is typically an instance of
	  the class set as AnimBlueprintGeneratedClass (generated by an animation blueprint)
	  Since this instance is transient, it is not safe to be used during construction script

Functions.Name: ClearDirtySubAnimInstances
Functions.Description:
清理所有脏标记的SubAnimInstance
	  从SubInstances、NewSubInstances、StopTickSubInstances中移除，并调用UninitializeAnimation、PendingDestroy等清理逻辑

Functions.Name: GetPostProcessInstance
Functions.Description:
Returns the active post process instance is one is available. This is set on the mesh that this
	  component is using, and is evaluated immediately after the main instance.

Functions.Name: SetAnimationMode
Functions.Description: Below are the interface to control animation when animation mode, not blueprint mode

Functions.Name: PlayAnimation
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	 
	  These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	  Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	  Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: SetAnimation
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: Play
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: Stop
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: IsPlaying
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: SetPosition
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: GetPosition
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: SetPlayRate
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: GetPlayRate
Functions.Description:
Animation play functions
	
	 These changes status of animation instance, which is transient data, which means it won't serialize with this component
	 Because of that reason, it is not safe to be used during construction script
	 Please use OverrideAnimationDatat for construction script. That will override AnimationData to be serialized

Functions.Name: OverrideAnimationData
Functions.Description:
This overrides current AnimationData parameter in the SkeletalMeshComponent. This will serialize when the component serialize
	  so it can be used during construction script. However note that this will override current existing data
	  This can be useful if you'd like to make a blueprint with custom default animation per component
	  This sets single player mode, which means you can't use AnimBlueprint with it

Functions.Name: SetMorphTarget
Functions.Description: Set Morph Target with Name and Value(0-1)

Params.Name: bRemoveZeroWeight
Params.Description: : Used by editor code when it should stay in the active list with zero weight

Functions.Name: ClearMorphTargets
Functions.Description: Clear all Morph Target that are set to this mesh

Functions.Name: GetMorphTarget
Functions.Description: Get Morph target with given name

Functions.Name: SnapshotPose
Functions.Description:
Takes a snapshot of this skeletal mesh component's pose and saves it to the specified snapshot.
	  The snapshot is taken at the current LOD, so if for example you took the snapshot at LOD1
	  and then used it at LOD0 any bones not in LOD1 will use the reference pose

Functions.Name: GetClothMaxDistanceScale
Functions.Description: GetSet the max distance scale of clothing mesh vertices

Functions.Name: ForceClothNextUpdateTeleport
Functions.Description:
Used to indicate we should force 'teleport' during the next call to UpdateClothState,
	  This will transform positions and velocities and thus keep the simulation state, just translate it to a new pose.

Functions.Name: ForceClothNextUpdateTeleportAndReset
Functions.Description:
Used to indicate we should force 'teleport and reset' during the next call to UpdateClothState.
	  This can be used to reset it from a bad state or by a teleport where the old state is not important anymore.

Functions.Name: SuspendClothingSimulation
Functions.Description: Stops simulating clothing, but does not show clothing ref pose. Keeps the last known simulation state

Functions.Name: ResumeClothingSimulation
Functions.Description: Resumes a previously suspended clothing simulation, teleporting the clothing on the next tick

Functions.Name: IsClothingSimulationSuspended
Functions.Description: Gets whether or not the clothing simulation is currently suspended

Functions.Name: ResetClothTeleportMode
Functions.Description: Reset the teleport mode of a next update to 'Continuous'

Functions.Name: BindClothToMasterPoseComponent
Functions.Description:
If this component has a valid MasterPoseComponent then this function makes cloth items on the slave component
	  take the transforms of the cloth items on the master component instead of simulating separately.

Functions.Name: UnbindClothFromMasterPoseComponent
Functions.Description:
If this component has a valid MasterPoseComponent and has previously had its cloth bound to the
	  MCP, this function will unbind the cloth and resume simulation.

Params.Name: bRestoreSimulationSpace
Params.Description: if true and the master pose cloth was originally simulating in world

Functions.Name: SetUpdateAnimationInEditor
Functions.Description:
Sets whether or not to force tick component in order to update animation and refresh transform for this component
	 This is supported only in the editor

Functions.Name: ResetAllowedAnimCurveEvaluation
Functions.Description: By reset, it will allow all the curves to be evaluated

Functions.Name: SetAllowedAnimCurvesEvaluation
Functions.Description: resets, and then only allow the following list to be alloweddisallowed

Functions.Name: GetTeleportRotationThreshold
Functions.Description: Gets the teleportation rotation threshold.

Functions.Name: SetTeleportRotationThreshold
Functions.Description: Sets the teleportation rotation threshold.

Params.Name: Threshold
Params.Description: Threshold in degrees.

Functions.Name: GetTeleportDistanceThreshold
Functions.Description: Gets the teleportation distance threshold.

Functions.Name: SetTeleportDistanceThreshold
Functions.Description: Sets the teleportation distance threshold.

Params.Name: Threshold
Params.Description: Threshold value.

Functions.Name: SetBodyNotifyRigidBodyCollision
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bNotifyRigidBodyCollision for a given body

Params.Name: bNewNotifyRigidBodyCollision
Params.Description: The value to assign to bNotifyRigidBodyCollision

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: Name of the body to turn hit notifies onoff. None implies root body

Functions.Name: SetNotifyRigidBodyCollisionBelow
Functions.Description: Changes the value of bNotifyRigidBodyCollision on all bodies below a given bone

Params.Name: bNewNotifyRigidBodyCollision
Params.Description: The value to assign to bNotifyRigidBodyCollision

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: Name of the body to turn hit notifies on (and below)

Params.Name: bIncludeSelf
Params.Description: Whether to modify the given body (useful for roots with multiple children)

Functions.Name: SetEnableBodyGravity
Functions.Description:
Enables or disables gravity for the given bone.
	 	NAME_None indicates the root body will be edited.
	 	If the bone name given is otherwise invalid, nothing happens.

Params.Name: bEnableGravity
Params.Description: Whether gravity should be enabled or disabled.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: The name of the bone to modify.

Functions.Name: IsBodyGravityEnabled
Functions.Description:
Checks whether or not gravity is enabled on the given bone.
	 	NAME_None indicates the root body should be queried.
	 	If the bone name given is otherwise invalid, false is returned.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: The name of the bone to check.

Return.Description: True if gravity is enabled on the bone.

Functions.Name: SetEnableGravityOnAllBodiesBelow
Functions.Description:
Enables or disables gravity to all bodies below the given bone.
	   NAME_None indicates all bodies will be edited.
		In that case, consider using UPrimitiveComponent::EnableGravity.

Params.Name: bEnableGravity
Params.Description: Whether gravity should be enabled or disabled.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: The name of the top most bone.

Params.Name: bIncludeSelf
Params.Description: Whether the bone specified should be edited.

Functions.Name: K2_GetClosestPointOnPhysicsAsset
Functions.Description: Given a world position, find the closest point on the physics asset. Note that this is independent of collision and welding. This is based purely on animation position

Params.Name: WorldPosition
Params.Description: The point we want the closest point to (i.e. for all bodies in the physics asset, find the one that has a point closest to WorldPosition)

Return.Description: true if we found a closest point

Functions.Name: GetBoneMass
Functions.Description: Returns the mass (in kg) of the given bone

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: Name of the body to return. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bScaleMass
Params.Description: If true, the mass is scaled by the bone's MassScale.

Functions.Name: GetSkeletalCenterOfMass
Functions.Description: Returns the center of mass of the skeletal mesh, instead of the root body's location

Functions.Name: AddForceToAllBodiesBelow
Functions.Description:
Add a force to all rigid bodies below.
	   This is like a 'thruster'. Good for adding a burst over some (non zero) time. Should be called every frame for the duration of the force.

Params.Name: Force
Params.Description: Force vector to apply. Magnitude indicates strength of force.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply force to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bAccelChange
Params.Description: If true, Force is taken as a change in acceleration instead of a physical force (i.e. mass will have no affect).

Params.Name: bIncludeSelf
Params.Description: If false, Force is only applied to bodies below but not given bone name.

Functions.Name: AddImpulseToAllBodiesBelow
Functions.Description: Add impulse to all single rigid bodies below. Good for one time instant burst.

Params.Name: Impulse
Params.Description: Magnitude and direction of impulse to apply.

Params.Name: BoneName
Params.Description: If a SkeletalMeshComponent, name of body to apply impulse to. 'None' indicates root body.

Params.Name: bVelChange
Params.Description: If true, the Strength is taken as a change in velocity instead of an impulse (ie. mass will have no affect).

Params.Name: bIncludeSelf
Params.Description: If false, Force is only applied to bodies below but not given bone name.

Functions.Name: SetAllBodiesSimulatePhysics
Functions.Description: Set bSimulatePhysics to true for all bone bodies. Does not change the component bSimulatePhysics flag.

Functions.Name: SetPhysicsBlendWeight
Functions.Description:
This is global set up for setting physics blend weight
	  This does multiple things automatically
	  If PhysicsBlendWeight == 1.f, it will enable Simulation, and if PhysicsBlendWeight == 0.f, it will disable Simulation.
	  Also it will respect each body's setup, so if the body is fixed, it won't simulate. Vice versa
	  So if you'd like all bodies to change manually, do not use this function, but SetAllBodiesPhysicsBlendWeight

Functions.Name: SetEnablePhysicsBlending
Functions.Description: Disable physics blending of bones

Functions.Name: SetAllBodiesBelowSimulatePhysics
Functions.Description: Set all of the bones below passed in bone to be simulated

Functions.Name: ResetAllBodiesSimulatePhysics
Functions.Description: Allows you to reset bodies Simulate state based on where bUsePhysics is set to true in the BodySetup.

Functions.Name: SetAllBodiesBelowPhysicsBlendWeight
Functions.Description: Set all of the bones below passed in bone to be simulated

Functions.Name: AccumulateAllBodiesBelowPhysicsBlendWeight
Functions.Description: Accumulate AddPhysicsBlendWeight to physics blendweight for all of the bones below passed in bone to be simulated

Functions.Name: SetAllMotorsAngularPositionDrive
Functions.Description: Enable or Disable AngularPositionDrive. If motor is in SLERP mode it will be turned on if either EnableSwingDrive OR EnableTwistDrive are enabled. In Twist and Swing mode the twist and the swing can be controlled individually.

Functions.Name: SetAllMotorsAngularVelocityDrive
Functions.Description: Enable or Disable AngularVelocityDrive. If motor is in SLERP mode it will be turned on if either EnableSwingDrive OR EnableTwistDrive are enabled. In Twist and Swing mode the twist and the swing can be controlled individually.

Functions.Name: SetAllMotorsAngularDriveParams
Functions.Description: Set Angular Drive motors params for all constraint instances

Functions.Name: SetConstraintProfile
Functions.Description: Sets the constraint profile properties (limits, motors, etc...) to match the constraint profile as defined in the physics asset. If profile name is not found the joint is set to use the default constraint profile.

Functions.Name: SetConstraintProfileForAll
Functions.Description: Sets the constraint profile properties (limits, motors, etc...) to match the constraint profile as defined in the physics asset for all constraints. If profile name is not found the joint is set to use the default constraint profile.

Functions.Name: FindConstraintBoneName
Functions.Description: Find Constraint Name from index

Params.Name: ConstraintIndex
Params.Description: Index of constraint to look for

Functions.Name: BreakConstraint
Functions.Description: Break a constraint off a Gore mesh.

Params.Name: Impulse
Params.Description: vector of impulse

Params.Name: HitLocation
Params.Description: location of the hit

Params.Name: InBoneName
Params.Description: Name of bone to break constraint for

Functions.Name: SetAngularLimits
Functions.Description: Sets the Angular Motion Ranges for a named bone

Params.Name: InBoneName
Params.Description: Name of bone to adjust constraint ranges for

Params.Name: Swing1LimitAngle
Params.Description: Size of limit in degrees, 0 means locked, 180 means free

Params.Name: TwistLimitAngle
Params.Description: Size of limit in degrees, 0 means locked, 180 means free

Params.Name: Swing2LimitAngle
Params.Description: Size of limit in degrees, 0 means locked, 180 means free

Functions.Name: GetCurrentJointAngles
Functions.Description: Gets the current Angular state for a named bone constraint

Params.Name: InBoneName
Params.Description: Name of bone to get constraint ranges for

Params.Name: Swing1Angle
Params.Description: current angular state of the constraint

Params.Name: TwistAngle
Params.Description: current angular state of the constraint

Params.Name: Swing2Angle
Params.Description: current angular state of the constraint

Functions.Name: HandleExistingParallelIMPhysicsEvaluationTask
Functions.Description: ImmediatePhysics Evaluation Start

Functions.Name: GetLastPoseTickFrame_BP
Functions.Description: Checked whether we have already ticked the pose this frame

Functions.Name: MarkDynamicBoneScaleFeature
Functions.Description:
For Bone Retarget Feature End 
	 
	
	  For Dynamic Bone Scale Feature Start
